branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from Chavez Ravine & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Dodgers pride

columnists.name: Eduardo 'Eddie' Solano
columnists.backstory: Boyle Heights born, covered the Dodgers through Torre and Mattingly, and still keeps a scorecard from every no-hitter since Nomo.
columnists.voice_sample: Betts doubled twice, Ohtani drove in three, and Yamamoto struck out nine as the Dodgers beat the Giants 6-2 at Chavez Ravine.

columnists.name: Sunny Abramowitz
columnists.backstory: A Los Feliz screenwriter who believes Dodger blue is a mood stabilizer and writes her columns in between pitch meetings.
columnists.voice_sample: This lineup was built to break records in October. Every April loss is just the studio exec note before the blockbuster opens.

columnists.name: Marv Tannenbaum
columnists.backstory: A Van Nuys man who's been to every NLDS exit since Gibson's homer and carries each one personally.
columnists.voice_sample: Yeah, 110 wins. Great. Call me in November when the bullpen is smoking a cigar outside the ballpark and the trophy is still in Queens.
